messages.show_one_week_of_timelog_stats_grouped_b: Show one week of timelog stats grouped by area
messages.show_one_year_of_timelog_stats_grouped_b: Show one year of timelog stats grouped by area
messages.show_schedule_views_grouped_by_local_dat: Show schedule views grouped by local date.
messages.show_subtree_completion_and_effort_stati: Show subtree completion and effort statistics for one task.
messages.show_task_counts_and_effort_totals_for_o: Show task counts and effort totals for one vision.
messages.show_the_aggregated_schedule_for_one_loc: Show the aggregated schedule for one local date or an inclusive range.
Show the aggregated schedule for one local day.
messages.show_the_program_version_and_exit_a7a672: Show the program version and exit
messages.single_record_mode_requires_both_start_t: Single-record mode requires both `--start-time` and `--end-time` because the record models completed time spent.
messages.single_resource_imports_expect_reference: Single-resource imports expect referenced foreign rows to already exist.
messages.start_date_of_the_enclosing_planning_cyc: Start date of the enclosing planning cycle window in YYYY-MM-DD format
messages.start_from_data_export_output_when_prepa: Start from `data export` output when preparing machine-generated patch rows.
messages.stats_are_grouped_only_by_area_task_effo: Stats are grouped only by area; task effort remains a separate task feature.
messages.synchronize_experience_points_from_root_: Synchronize experience points from root task actual effort totals.
messages.tags_are_intended_for_structured_labelin: Tags are intended for structured labeling, not free-form note content.
messages.tags_provide_cross_cutting_classificatio: Tags provide cross-cutting classification across otherwise separate domains.
messages.target_local_date_in_yyyy_mm_dd_format_d: Target local date in YYYY-MM-DD format; defaults to today when omitted
messages.task_order_in_task_id_display_order_form: Task order in <task-id>:<display-order> format; repeat for multiple tasks
messages.task_rows_come_from_planning_cycle_overl: Task rows come from planning-cycle overlap, not from event timeblocks.
messages.tasks_appear_when_the_local_date_falls_i: Tasks appear when the local date falls inside their planning-cycle window. Overdue unfinished tasks and habit actions also roll forward into non-future schedule days. Events appear when their scheduled time overlaps that day.
messages.tasks_are_the_main_execution_unit_in_lif: Tasks are the main execution unit in LifeOS and can be nested under parent tasks.
messages.tasks_can_be_root_tasks_or_child_tasks_u: Tasks can be root tasks or child tasks under another task in the same vision.
messages.text_to_find_in_each_target_note_e147da5: Text to find in each target note
messages.the_effective_hourly_rate_comes_from_the: The effective hourly rate comes from the vision override when set, otherwise from preferences.
messages.the_output_groups_tasks_habit_actions_an: The output groups tasks, habit actions, and event occurrences for the day.
messages.the_output_starts_with_the_vision_identi: The output starts with the vision identifier, then prints each root task and descendant using the same indented task tree shape as `with-subtasks`.
messages.the_root_task_prints_first_followed_by_a: The root task prints first, followed by active descendants indented by depth with columns: task_id, status, completion_percentage, content.
messages.the_week_boundary_follows_the_configured: The week boundary follows the configured `week_starts_on` preference.
messages.these_commands_operate_on_the_database_c: These commands operate on the database configured through `lifeos init`, the config file, or LIFEOS_* environment variables.
messages.these_metrics_are_derived_from_cadence_s: These metrics are derived from cadence settings together with materialized `habit-action` records.
messages.this_command_changes_only_display_order_: This command changes only `display_order`; it does not move tasks between parents.
messages.this_command_follows_the_same_editable_w: This command follows the same editable-window rules as `update`.
messages.this_command_is_the_recommended_first_st: This command is the recommended first step after installing lifeos-cli.
messages.this_command_mirrors_lifeos_note_delete_: This command mirrors `lifeos note delete`, but works across many note IDs.
messages.this_command_only_allows_status_and_note: This command only allows status and notes changes within the editable window.
messages.this_command_succeeds_only_when_the_visi: This command succeeds only when the vision is active and already at final stage.
messages.this_command_writes_the_config_file_not_: This command writes the config file, not environment variables.
messages.this_is_the_first_batch_editing_primitiv: This is the first batch-editing primitive for notes and provides a base shape for future bulk operations.
messages.this_namespace_currently_exposes_only_th: This namespace currently exposes only the `delete` workflow.
messages.this_namespace_uses_canonical_json_jsonl: This namespace uses canonical JSON/JSONL snapshot rows instead of ad-hoc CLI flags.
messages.this_resource_is_for_named_people_relati: This resource is for named people, relationship context, and explicit execution subjects.
messages.this_updates_schema_state_in_the_databas: This updates schema state in the database; it does not rewrite local config.
messages.timelogs_can_optionally_reference_one_ar: Timelogs can optionally reference one area and one task.
messages.timelogs_represent_what_really_happened_: Timelogs represent what really happened and how time was spent.
messages.toggle_whether_the_area_is_active_0ca170: Toggle whether the area is active
messages.totals_aggregate_the_selected_task_toget: Totals aggregate the selected task together with all active descendants.
messages.update_a_habit_action_by_date_7f64e2ac: Update a habit action by date
messages.update_display_order_values_for_one_or_m: Update display order values for one or more tasks.
messages.update_mutable_fields_across_multiple_ac: Update mutable fields across multiple active timelogs.
messages.update_note_content_and_weak_association: Update note content and weak associations in place.
messages.update_scope_for_recurring_events_single: Update scope for recurring events: single, all_future, or all
messages.updated_allowed_weekdays_for_example_mon: Updated allowed weekdays, for example monday,wednesday,friday
messages.updated_birth_date_in_iso_format_yyyy_mm: Updated birth date in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD
messages.updated_cadence_frequency_daily_weekly_m: Updated cadence frequency: daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly
messages.updated_cadence_target_count_for_one_cyc: Updated cadence target count for one cycle
messages.updated_event_type_appointment_timeblock: Updated event type: appointment, timeblock, or deadline
messages.updated_habit_start_date_in_yyyy_mm_dd_f: Updated habit start date in YYYY-MM-DD format
messages.updated_planning_cycle_duration_in_days_: Updated planning cycle duration in days for the enclosing timebox
messages.updated_planning_cycle_fields_still_desc: Updated planning-cycle fields still describe the enclosing timebox, not a specific scheduled timestamp.
messages.updated_planning_cycle_type_year_month_w: Updated planning cycle type: year, month, week, or day
messages.updated_recurrence_frequency_daily_weekl: Updated recurrence frequency: daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly
messages.updated_start_date_of_the_enclosing_plan: Updated start date of the enclosing planning cycle window in YYYY-MM-DD format
messages.use_a_case_sensitive_find_replace_instea: Use a case-sensitive find/replace instead of case-insensitive matching
messages.use_active_window_only_to_show_habits_wh: Use `--active-window-only` to show habits whose duration still covers today.
messages.use_areas_to_group_visions_and_other_hig: Use areas to group visions and other higher-level planning objects.
messages.use_cadence_frequency_to_choose_daily_we: Use `--cadence-frequency` to choose daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly evaluation cycles.
messages.use_clear_description_clear_color_or_cle: Use `--clear-description`, `--clear-color`, or `--clear-people` to remove optional values.
messages.use_clear_description_or_clear_icon_to_r: Use `--clear-description` or `--clear-icon` to remove optional values.
messages.use_clear_description_or_clear_task_to_r: Use `--clear-description` or `--clear-task` to remove optional values.
messages.use_clear_flags_to_remove_optional_links: Use `--clear-*` flags to remove optional links.
messages.use_clear_flags_to_remove_optional_value: Use `--clear-*` flags to remove optional values, including people.
Use `--clear-*` flags to remove optional values instead of replacing them.
Use `--clear-*` flags to remove optional values such as descriptions or planning cycles.
messages.use_clear_parent_to_move_a_child_task_ba: Use `--clear-parent` to move a child task back to the root level.
messages.use_clear_weekdays_to_remove_weekday_res: Use `--clear-weekdays` to remove weekday restrictions without resetting cadence.
messages.use_config_set_for_supported_follow_up_e: Use `config set` for supported follow-up edits when you do not need to re-run the full bootstrap flow.
messages.use_config_set_to_persist_one_supported_: Use `config set` to persist one supported key after reviewing the current values.
messages.use_config_show_to_inspect_the_effective: Use `config show` to inspect the effective values after environment overrides.
messages.use_db_ping_first_if_you_are_unsure_the_: Use `db ping` first if you are unsure the current connection settings are correct.
messages.use_delete_to_remove_multiple_notes_by_i: Use `delete` to remove multiple notes by identifier.
messages.use_delete_to_remove_multiple_timelogs_i: Use `delete` to remove multiple timelogs in one command.
messages.use_dry_run_before_applying_a_large_file: Use `--dry-run` before applying a large file or a full bundle restore.
messages.use_dry_run_before_applying_large_import: Use `--dry-run` before applying large imports or batch changes.
messages.use_entity_type_and_category_to_narrow_t: Use entity type and category to narrow the result set before inspecting one tag in detail.
messages.use_export_output_as_the_round_trip_cont: Use `export` output as the round-trip contract for later `import` or `batch-update`.
messages.use_filters_and_pagination_flags_here_in: Use filters and pagination flags here instead of expecting a separate search command.
messages.use_hierarchy_when_you_need_the_full_act: Use `hierarchy` when you need the full active tree for an entire vision.
messages.use_import_bundle_for_full_backup_restor: Use `import bundle` for full backup restore. Bundle restore is atomic and restores base rows before relation links.
messages.use_inactive_when_the_area_should_exist_: Use `--inactive` when the area should exist but not appear as active yet.
messages.use_include_deleted_to_inspect_a_deleted: Use `--include-deleted` to inspect a deleted area.
Use `--include-deleted` to inspect a deleted note.
messages.use_include_deleted_to_review_previously: Use `--include-deleted` to review previously deleted tags.
messages.use_it_for_the_human_partner_and_when_us: Use it for the human partner and, when useful, a named automation identity so work ownership stays explicit.
messages.use_lifeos_event_add_task_id_task_id_if_: Use `lifeos event add --task-id <task-id>` if the task also needs a specific time block on a calendar day.
messages.use_lifeos_event_add_task_id_task_id_whe: Use `lifeos event add --task-id <task-id>` when the task also needs a specific appointment, timeblock, or deadline.
messages.use_lifeos_init_for_first_time_bootstrap: Use `lifeos init` for first-time bootstrap or when changing the schema binding.
messages.use_lifeos_init_schema_name_to_change_th: Use `lifeos init --schema <name>` to change the database schema binding.
messages.use_lifeos_resource_help_and_lifeos_reso: Use `lifeos <resource> --help` and `lifeos <resource> <action> --help` as the primary command reference.
messages.use_list_as_the_primary_query_entrypoint: Use `list` as the primary query entrypoint for events.
Use `list` as the primary query entrypoint for habits.
Use `list` as the primary query entrypoint for this resource.
Use `list` as the primary query entrypoint for timelogs.
messages.use_list_for_both_per_habit_and_by_date_: Use `list` for both per-habit and by-date views.
messages.use_list_when_you_need_the_same_schedule: Use `list` when you need the same schedule view across an inclusive date range.
messages.use_log_to_materialize_and_update_an_occ: Use `log` to materialize and update an occurrence by habit and date.
messages.use_log_when_you_know_the_habit_and_date: Use `log` when you know the habit and date but have not looked up the action ID.
messages.use_move_when_parentage_or_vision_member: Use `move` when parentage or vision membership also needs to change.
messages.use_old_parent_task_id_as_an_optimistic_: Use `--old-parent-task-id` as an optimistic guard when another writer may have already moved the task.
messages.use_ping_to_validate_the_current_connect: Use `ping` to validate the current connection settings without changing schema.
messages.use_planning_cycle_fields_for_the_broade: Use planning-cycle fields for the broader timebox. Use `event` when the task also needs a concrete time block.
Use planning-cycle fields for the broader year, month, week, or day window that the task belongs to.
messages.use_query_for_lightweight_text_filtering: Use `--query` for lightweight text filtering across titles and notes.
messages.use_range_for_arbitrary_windows_and_rebu: Use `range` for arbitrary windows and `rebuild` to refresh persisted stats.
messages.use_rebuild_after_upgrading_older_datase: Use rebuild after upgrading older datasets or importing historical timelogs.
messages.use_recurrence_flags_to_create_recurring: Use recurrence flags to create recurring daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly series.
messages.use_relation_flags_without_content_when_: Use relation flags without `content` when only links need to change.
messages.use_reorder_when_only_sibling_display_or: Use `reorder` when only sibling display order changes and parentage stays the same.
messages.use_repeated_person_id_to_keep_actual_hu: Use repeated `--person-id` to keep actual human effort, agent effort, and shared effort distinct.
messages.use_repeated_person_id_to_keep_human_onl: Use repeated `--person-id` to keep human-only, agent-only, and shared task ownership explicit.
Use repeated `--person-id` to keep human-only, agent-only, and shared ownership explicit as plans change.
messages.use_resource_specific_commands_for_highe: Use resource-specific commands for higher-level behaviors such as recurring event occurrence scopes.
messages.use_resource_specific_delete_commands_wh: Use resource-specific delete commands when you want narrower human-guided changes.
messages.use_set_to_persist_supported_keys_into_t: Use `set` to persist supported keys into the local config file.
messages.use_set_to_persist_supported_keys_withou: Use `set` to persist supported keys without re-running the full init flow.
messages.use_show_for_one_exact_local_day_067d48f: Use `show` for one exact local day.
messages.use_show_to_inspect_effective_values_aft: Use `show` to inspect effective values after config-file and env resolution.
messages.use_show_when_you_also_need_the_underlyi: Use `show` when you also need the underlying habit fields in the same output.
messages.use_show_when_you_want_the_single_day_en: Use `show` when you want the single-day entrypoint with the same sections.
messages.use_stats_for_timelog_stats_grouped_by_a: Use `stats` for timelog stats grouped by area.
messages.use_stdin_or_file_for_multi_line_note_co: Use `--stdin` or `--file` for multi-line note content.
messages.use_sync_experience_when_experience_shou: Use `sync-experience` when experience should be recomputed from task effort.
messages.use_tags_nicknames_and_location_to_captu: Use tags, nicknames, and location to capture relationship context or an execution subject for task, event, or timelog ownership.
messages.use_this_action_to_capture_short_thought: Use this action to capture short thoughts, prompts, or raw text before linking them to tasks, people, or timelogs.
messages.use_this_action_when_you_need_exact_fiel: Use this action when you need exact field values instead of the compact list view.
messages.use_this_action_when_you_need_to_inspect: Use this action when you need to inspect preserved line breaks instead of the single-line summary from `note list`.
messages.use_this_after_task_effort_changes_when_: Use this after task effort changes when vision experience should match current root-task actual effort.
messages.use_this_as_the_primary_query_entrypoint: Use this as the primary query entrypoint for people rather than expecting a separate search command.
Use this as the primary query entrypoint for visions.
messages.use_this_before_db_upgrade_when_you_firs: Use this before `db upgrade` when you first need to confirm the target database.

messages.use_this_command_for_file_driven_or_mach: Use this command for file-driven or machine-generated cleanup across many rows.
messages.use_this_command_to_audit_which_active_h: Use this command to audit which active habits are still attached to tasks.
messages.use_this_command_to_inspect_daily_occurr: Use this command to inspect daily occurrence views and materialized records.
messages.use_this_command_when_checking_in_withou: Use this command when checking in without first looking up the action ID.
messages.use_this_for_explicit_manual_credit_rath: Use this for explicit manual credit rather than for task-effort recalculation.
messages.use_this_namespace_for_machine_oriented_: Use this namespace for machine-oriented bulk workflows such as export, import, batch update, batch delete, and full bundle backup or restore.
messages.use_title_contains_for_lightweight_text_: Use `--title-contains` for lightweight text filtering instead of a separate search command.
messages.use_type_to_narrow_results_to_one_event_: Use `--type` to narrow results to one event topology.
messages.use_type_to_retag_an_event_as_appointmen: Use `--type` to retag an event as appointment, timeblock, or deadline.
messages.use_update_content_for_bulk_find_replace: Use `update-content` for bulk find/replace across active note content.
messages.use_update_to_edit_mutable_fields_across: Use `update` to edit mutable fields across active timelogs.
messages.use_update_when_you_already_have_the_mat: Use `update` when you already have the materialized action identifier.
messages.use_upgrade_when_the_configured_database: Use `upgrade` when the configured database schema needs to catch up with the code.
messages.use_with_counts_to_add_relationship_coun: Use `--with-counts` to add relationship count columns: {columns}.
messages.use_with_stats_when_the_summary_should_i: Use `--with-stats` when the summary should include cycle progress and streak fields.
messages.use_with_subtasks_when_you_want_to_inspe: Use `with-subtasks` when you want to inspect only one branch of the tree.
messages.use_with_tasks_when_you_need_the_row_lev: Use `with-tasks` when you need the row-level task list instead of totals.
messages.visions_are_intended_to_group_related_ta: Visions are intended to group related task trees.
messages.visions_usually_represent_medium_or_long: Visions usually represent medium- or long-running themes that will own multiple tasks.
messages.weekdays_restricts_on_demand_habit_actio: `--weekdays` restricts on-demand habit-action occurrences to selected weekdays.
messages.welcome_bug_reports_and_suggestions_thro: Welcome bug reports and suggestions through https://github.com/liujuanjuan1984/lifeos-cli.
messages.when_an_agent_creates_events_for_a_human: When an agent creates events for a human, use `--person-id` to keep human-only, agent-only, and shared time blocks distinct.
messages.when_an_agent_creates_tasks_on_behalf_of: When an agent creates tasks on behalf of a human, use `--person-id` to mark whether the task belongs to the human, the agent, or both.
messages.when_an_agent_records_actual_work_use_pe: When an agent records actual work, use `--person-id` to state whether the effort belongs to the human, the agent, or both.
messages.when_both_start_time_and_end_time_are_gi: When both `--start-time` and `--end-time` are given, overlapping events are returned.
messages.when_date_is_omitted_show_uses_the_curre: When `--date` is omitted, `show` uses the current configured local date.
messages.when_quick_batch_mode_omits_first_start_: When quick batch mode omits `--first-start-time`, the first row inherits the latest active timelog end time.
messages.when_results_exist_the_command_prints_a_: When results exist, the command prints a header row followed by tab-separated columns: {columns}.
messages.when_results_exist_the_list_command_prin: When results exist, the list command prints a header row followed by tab-separated columns: {columns}.
messages.when_the_task_has_direct_children_comple: When the task has direct children, `completion_percentage` measures how many of those children are done.
messages.when_the_vision_has_tasks_the_tasks_sect: When the vision has tasks, the `tasks` section prints a header row followed by tab-separated columns: {columns}.
messages.when_vision_id_is_provided_without_paren: When `--vision-id` is provided without `--parent-task-id`, only root tasks are listed.
messages.wrap_inline_content_in_quotes_when_it_co: Wrap inline content in quotes when it contains spaces.
messages.write_exported_data_to_a_file_instead_of: Write exported data to a file instead of stdout
messages.write_one_supported_config_key_to_the_lo: Write one supported config key to the local config file.
messages.write_quick_batch_timelogs_without_inter: Write quick batch timelogs without interactive confirmation after preview
